tasks.title: Create LLM Integration Framework Foundation
tasks.description: Establish the core synthesis module structure and abstract base classes for LLM provider integration following industry-standard patterns
tasks.details: Create the synthesis/ directory structure with __init__.py, synthesizer.py, models.py, prompts.py, and validators.py. Implement BaseLLMProvider abstract class with generate() and get_token_count() methods. Set up the foundation for the Abstract Provider Pattern to support multiple LLM providers (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini). Include proper error handling interfaces and async support preparation.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for abstract base class instantiation, method signature validation, and proper inheritance patterns. Mock provider implementations to verify interface compliance.

subtasks.title: Create `synthesis/providers` directory structure
subtasks.description: Create the directory structure `src/synthesis/providers/` to house individual provider implementations. This keeps the provider-specific logic organized and separate from the core synthesis engine.

subtasks.title: Relocate BaseLLMProvider to providers/base.py
subtasks.description: Move the existing `BaseLLMProvider` abstract class from `src/synthesis/base.py` to a new file at `src/synthesis/providers/base.py` to serve as the central interface for all LLM providers.

subtasks.title: Create NoOpProvider for default behavior
subtasks.description: Create a `NoOpProvider` class in `src/synthesis/providers/noop.py` that inherits from `BaseLLMProvider`. It will serve as the default provider when AI synthesis is disabled, returning the input content without modification.

subtasks.title: Implement ProviderFactory
subtasks.description: Implement a `ProviderFactory` in a new file `src/synthesis/factory.py`. This factory will be responsible for dynamically selecting and instantiating the correct LLM provider based on the settings in the Config object.

subtasks.title: Pre-implementation Validation: Review framework design
subtasks.description: Before implementation, review the planned `BaseLLMProvider` abstraction and `ProviderFactory` design against the technical specifications and patterns outlined in the PRD to ensure they meet requirements.

subtasks.title: Post-implementation Validation: Write unit tests for ProviderFactory
subtasks.description: After implementation, write unit tests for the `ProviderFactory`. The tests should cover dynamic provider selection, instantiation of all supported providers (including the NoOpProvider), and handling of invalid configurations.

tasks.title: Implement Ollama Local LLM Provider
tasks.description: Create the Ollama provider implementation for local LLM deployment with model management and caching capabilities
tasks.details: Implement OllamaProvider class inheriting from BaseLLMProvider. Add HTTP client integration with ollama API (default: http://localhost:11434). Implement model management (list, pull, run), response streaming, and basic caching. Include connection validation, model availability checks, and proper error handling for network issues. Add token counting approximation for local models.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration tests with mock Ollama server, unit tests for model management functions, error handling validation for network failures and model unavailability. Test caching mechanisms and token counting accuracy.

subtasks.title: Research Python Ollama Integration Best Practices
subtasks.description: Using the `Context7` and `firecrawl_search` tools, research the best practices for integrating with Ollama in Python. Focus on the `langchain-community` package's Ollama integration, as mentioned in the PRD, and identify the recommended patterns for API interaction and error handling.

subtasks.title: Create OllamaProvider class structure
subtasks.description: Create the `OllamaProvider` class in a new file at `src/synthesis/providers/ollama.py`. This class should inherit from `BaseLLMProvider` and be initialized with the necessary configuration for connecting to a local Ollama instance.
subtasks.details:
<info added on 2025-06-09T19:50:59.635Z>
Create the OllamaProvider class in src/synthesis/providers/ollama.py that inherits from BaseLLMProvider. Use httpx for asynchronous HTTP requests to communicate with the Ollama API at the configured base URL and model. Implement the async generate method to handle text generation requests and the get_token_count method using HuggingFace tokenizer for accurate token counting. All configuration parameters should be sourced from the config system. Include comprehensive docstrings explaining the class purpose, method parameters, and return values. Add proper type annotations for all methods and parameters to ensure type safety and code clarity.
</info added on 2025-06-09T19:50:59.635Z>

subtasks.title: Implement the `generate()` method
subtasks.description: Implement the `generate()` method within the `OllamaProvider` class. This method should handle the construction of the request, making the API call to the Ollama server, and processing the response, including support for streaming. It should be wrapped with our resilience decorator.
subtasks.details:
<info added on 2025-06-09T19:53:06.102Z>
Implement the async generate() method that sends POST requests to the Ollama HTTP API endpoint using httpx client. The method should retrieve the base URL and model name from the provider configuration, construct the appropriate API request payload, and handle the HTTP communication with proper error handling for network issues and API errors. Parse the JSON response from Ollama to extract the generated text content and return it as a string. Include comprehensive docstrings documenting the method parameters, return values, and potential exceptions that may be raised during execution.
</info added on 2025-06-09T19:53:06.102Z>

subtasks.title: Implement the `get_token_count()` method
subtasks.description: Implement the `get_token_count()` method for the `OllamaProvider`. Based on research, this might involve using a local tokenizer as an approximation, as Ollama itself may not provide a dedicated token counting endpoint. Document this approximation.
subtasks.details:
<info added on 2025-06-09T19:53:17.107Z>
Implement the get_token_count() method in OllamaProvider class. Use the HuggingFace tokenizer from src.utils.get_tokenizer(config) to estimate token counts. Include proper type annotations and comprehensive docstrings explaining the method's purpose and limitations. Document that this implementation provides an approximation since Ollama does not expose a native token counting endpoint. The method should accept text input and return an integer representing the estimated token count.
</info added on 2025-06-09T19:53:17.107Z>

subtasks.title: Register OllamaProvider in ProviderFactory
subtasks.description: Update the `ProviderFactory` in `src/synthesis/factory.py` to recognize and instantiate the `OllamaProvider`. This will involve adding it to the factory's mapping of provider names to provider classes, enabling dynamic selection.
subtasks.details:
<info added on 2025-06-09T19:53:25.110Z>
Add registration logic to ProviderFactory class to instantiate OllamaProvider when config.llm_provider is set to 'ollama'. Update the factory method to include conditional check for ollama provider type and return OllamaProvider(config) instance. Import OllamaProvider class at top of factory module. Update factory class docstring to document ollama provider support. Design registration pattern to easily accommodate additional provider types in future without breaking existing functionality.
</info added on 2025-06-09T19:53:25.110Z>

subtasks.title: Pre-implementation Validation: Review Ollama implementation plan
subtasks.description: Before implementation, verify that the planned approach for the `OllamaProvider` aligns with the best practices discovered during the research subtask. Ensure the implementation plan correctly uses the `langchain-community` integration and handles potential API differences.

subtasks.title: Post-implementation Validation: Test OllamaProvider
subtasks.description: After implementation, write comprehensive unit tests for the `OllamaProvider`. Use mocking to simulate API responses from the Ollama server, and test success cases, error handling, and the token counting approximation.
subtasks.details:
<info added on 2025-06-09T19:53:34.741Z>
Create comprehensive unit tests for the OllamaProvider class in tests/test_ollama_provider.py. Use unittest.mock or pytest-asyncio to mock httpx.AsyncClient and the tokenizer to ensure tests run independently without requiring a running Ollama server.

Test coverage should include:
- generate method success scenarios with proper response handling
- generate method error scenarios including network failures and API errors
- get_token_count method functionality with mocked tokenizer
- Proper async/await handling throughout all test cases
- Fast execution times by avoiding any real network calls or external dependencies

All tests must be self-contained and use appropriate mocking to simulate Ollama API responses and tokenizer behavior.
</info added on 2025-06-09T19:53:34.741Z>

tasks.title: Develop Content Synthesis Engine
tasks.description: Create the core ContentSynthesizer class using LangChain LCEL patterns for chainable content enhancement operations
tasks.details: Implement ContentSynthesizer class with LangChain LCEL chain composition (ChatPromptTemplate | provider | StrOutputParser). Create synthesis methods for chunk enhancement while preserving document structure. Implement refinement levels (light, moderate, aggressive) with corresponding prompt templates. Add quality validation to ensure factual accuracy preservation and implement fallback mechanisms for synthesis failures.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for chain composition, integration tests with mock LLM responses, quality preservation validation through before/after content comparison, and refinement level effectiveness testing.

subtasks.title: Create the ContentSynthesizer class structure
subtasks.description: Create the main `ContentSynthesizer` class in a new file, `src/synthesis/engine.py`. This class will be initialized with an LLM provider instance (obtained from the factory) and will serve as the main entry point for all content synthesis operations.

subtasks.title: Implement the `refine_chunk()` method
subtasks.description: Implement the primary `refine_chunk()` method in the `ContentSynthesizer`. This method will take a content chunk and synthesis options (from the Config) as input, construct the appropriate prompt, and use the LLM provider to generate the refined content.

subtasks.title: Integrate PromptManager into ContentSynthesizer
subtasks.description: Integrate the `PromptManager` (from Task 4) into the `ContentSynthesizer`. The `refine_chunk()` method should use the `PromptManager` to dynamically select and format the correct prompt based on the document type and desired synthesis operation.

subtasks.title: Pre-implementation Validation: Review ContentSynthesizer design
subtasks.description: Before implementation, review the design of the `ContentSynthesizer` to ensure it follows a clean, single-responsibility principle. It should orchestrate the synthesis process, not get bogged down in the details of prompt management or provider communication.

subtasks.title: Post-implementation Validation: Test ContentSynthesizer
subtasks.description: After implementation, write unit tests for the `ContentSynthesizer`. Use a mocked LLM provider and a mocked `PromptManager` to verify that the synthesizer correctly coordinates the synthesis process and handles various inputs and configurations.

tasks.title: Create Prompt Engineering Framework
tasks.description: Develop format-specific prompt templates and engineering system for optimal content synthesis across different document types
tasks.details: Create PromptTemplateManager with format-specific templates for PDF, HTML, DOCX, PPTX, and Image content. Implement refinement-level specific prompts (light: grammar/clarity, moderate: structure improvement, aggressive: comprehensive rewriting). Add context-aware prompting using document metadata and chunk position. Include prompt validation and A/B testing framework for continuous improvement.
tasks.testStrategy: Template rendering tests, format-specific prompt validation, refinement level effectiveness measurement, and prompt injection security testing.

subtasks.title: Create prompt template directory structure
subtasks.description: Create a new directory at `src/synthesis/prompts/` to store all prompt templates. Inside, create subdirectories for each document format we support (e.g., `pdf/`, `html/`, `markdown/`), ensuring a scalable structure.

subtasks.title: Develop base prompt templates
subtasks.description: Develop a set of base prompt templates for common synthesis tasks, such as 'summarize', 'reformat', and 'correct_grammar'. Store these as `.txt` files in the new `prompts` directory, using a clear and consistent naming convention.

subtasks.title: Create the PromptManager class
subtasks.description: Create the `PromptManager` class in a new file, `src/synthesis/prompt_manager.py`. This class will be responsible for loading prompt templates from the file system, caching them, and providing an interface to retrieve and format them with dynamic data.

subtasks.title: Pre-implementation Validation: Review prompt framework design
subtasks.description: Before implementation, review the design of the `PromptManager` and the prompt template structure. Ensure the design supports easy addition of new prompts and format-specific overrides, as specified in the PRD.

subtasks.title: Post-implementation Validation: Test PromptManager
subtasks.description: After implementation, write comprehensive unit tests for the `PromptManager`. Use a temporary directory with mock prompt files to test prompt loading, caching, selection logic, and formatting with placeholder data.

tasks.title: Extend Configuration System for LLM Parameters
tasks.description: Enhance the existing Config dataclass with Phase 2 LLM synthesis parameters and provider-specific settings
tasks.details: Extend Config dataclass with enable_synthesis, llm_provider, llm_model, refinement_level, max_synthesis_tokens, preserve_structure parameters. Add provider-specific settings (API keys, base URLs, timeouts). Implement configuration validation, environment variable support, and configuration file loading. Include error handling configuration (max_retries, base_delay, circuit_breaker settings) based on research findings.
tasks.testStrategy: Configuration validation tests, environment variable loading verification, provider-specific setting validation, and configuration file parsing tests.

subtasks.title: Add provider settings to Config
subtasks.description: Add LLM provider settings to `src/config.py`, including `llm_provider`, `llm_model`, and provider-specific keys/URLs as optional fields.

subtasks.title: Add synthesis control flags to Config
subtasks.description: Add synthesis control flags like `refinement_level: str`, and `preserve_structure: bool` to the Config dataclass. The `refine: bool` flag already exists.

subtasks.title: Add network and error handling settings to Config
subtasks.description: Add network and error handling settings from PRD research, such as `max_retries`, `base_delay`, and `circuit_breaker_threshold`, to the Config dataclass.

subtasks.title: Update Config.validate() for Phase 2 parameters
subtasks.description: Update the `Config.validate()` method in `src/config.py` to include comprehensive checks for all new Phase 2 parameters (provider, synthesis, network).

subtasks.title: Pre-implementation Validation: Review new Config against PRD
subtasks.description: Before implementation, cross-reference all new configuration parameters with the 'Phase 2 Architecture Extensions' and 'Technical Implementation' sections of the PRD to ensure complete alignment.

subtasks.title: Post-implementation Validation: Write unit tests for Config
subtasks.description: After implementation, write comprehensive unit tests for the new validation logic in `Config` to verify that it correctly handles both valid and invalid Phase 2 settings.
subtasks.details:
<info added on 2025-06-09T19:32:49.921Z>
After implementation, write comprehensive unit tests for the new LLM synthesis and provider validation logic in Config to verify that it correctly handles both valid and invalid settings.
</info added on 2025-06-09T19:32:49.921Z>

tasks.title: Implement Quality Validation Framework
tasks.description: Create comprehensive validation system to ensure content accuracy preservation during AI synthesis
tasks.details: Implement ContentValidator class with factual accuracy checks, structure preservation validation, and information loss detection. Create metrics for readability improvement measurement and content quality scoring. Add validation rules for different refinement levels and implement rollback mechanisms for failed synthesis. Include semantic similarity checking and key information preservation verification.
tasks.testStrategy: Accuracy preservation tests with known content modifications, structure validation tests, information loss detection verification, and semantic similarity measurement validation.

tasks.title: Enhance CLI Interface with Synthesis Options
tasks.description: Extend the existing Typer-based CLI with --refine flag and synthesis-related command options
tasks.details: Add --refine flag to enable synthesis mode, --provider option for LLM provider selection (ollama, openai, anthropic, google), --model option for specific model selection, and --refinement-level option (light, moderate, aggressive). Implement progress indicators for synthesis operations, enhanced error messages for LLM-related failures, and validation for synthesis parameters. Add help text and examples for new options.
tasks.testStrategy: CLI argument parsing tests, option validation tests, help text verification, and integration tests for synthesis workflow activation.

subtasks.title: Add `--refine` flag to CLI
subtasks.description: In `src/main.py`, extend the main `convert` command with a new `--refine` flag. This flag will act as the primary switch to enable or disable the AI synthesis features for a conversion job. The flag already exists in the config.

subtasks.title: Expose synthesis options as CLI arguments
subtasks.description: Expose the new synthesis-related options from our `Config` dataclass as command-line arguments. This includes `--llm-provider`, `--llm-model`, `--refinement-level`, and other relevant parameters, allowing for fine-grained control from the CLI.

subtasks.title: Integrate ContentSynthesizer into main application flow
subtasks.description: In `main.py`, modify the main application logic to conditionally invoke the `ContentSynthesizer` after the initial document processing pipelines are complete. This should only happen if the `--refine` flag is active.

subtasks.title: Pre-implementation Validation: Review CLI changes
subtasks.description: Before implementation, review the planned CLI changes to ensure they are user-friendly and consistent with the existing command structure. Verify that all new options have clear names, help text, and sensible defaults.

subtasks.title: Post-implementation Validation: Test CLI enhancements
subtasks.description: After implementation, write unit tests for the CLI. Use a test runner like `CliRunner` to simulate command-line invocations with various combinations of the new synthesis flags and options, and verify that the application behaves correctly.

tasks.title: Implement Error Handling and Circuit Breaker Pattern
tasks.description: Create robust error handling system with circuit breaker pattern for LLM provider failures and comprehensive retry mechanisms
tasks.details: Implement CircuitBreaker class with configurable failure thresholds and timeout periods. Create comprehensive error classification (Network, Authentication, Rate Limiting, Input Validation) with specific handling strategies. Add exponential backoff with jitter for rate limiting, provider fallback mechanisms, and graceful degradation. Include error logging and user-friendly error messages for different failure scenarios.
tasks.testStrategy: Circuit breaker state transition tests, error classification validation, retry mechanism testing with mock failures, and fallback scenario verification.

subtasks.title: Define synthesis-specific exception classes
subtasks.description: In `src/exceptions.py`, define new exception classes that inherit from `LLMarkableError`: `SynthesisError`, `ProviderError`, `ProviderAuthenticationError`, and `RateLimitError` to handle specific failure modes in the synthesis process.

subtasks.title: Implement Circuit Breaker pattern
subtasks.description: Research and select a lightweight circuit breaker library (e.g., 'pybreaker') or implement a simple one from scratch in a new file, `src/synthesis/circuit_breaker.py`. The implementation should support failure thresholds and reset timeouts.

subtasks.title: Implement exponential backoff with jitter utility
subtasks.description: In a new `src/synthesis/utils.py` file, implement a utility function for exponential backoff with jitter. This function will be used to intelligently space out retries when API calls to LLM providers fail.

subtasks.title: Integrate resilience patterns into provider calls
subtasks.description: Integrate the circuit breaker and retry logic into the base LLM provider interaction flow. This could be done via a decorator in `synthesis/utils.py` that wraps the `generate` method calls, applying the resilience patterns consistently across all providers.

subtasks.title: Pre-implementation Validation: Review resilience patterns
subtasks.description: Before implementation, review the chosen resilience patterns (circuit breaker, exponential backoff) against industry best practices and the research outlined in the PRD to ensure a robust and maintainable design.
subtasks.details:
<info added on 2025-06-10T19:01:39.981Z>
Pre-implementation validation confirms that resilience patterns are properly architected with async-compatible, modular decorators and classes following 2025-06 best practices. Circuit breaker implementation is configurable, async-aware, and tracks only relevant exceptions. Exponential backoff with jitter is implemented for retries using config-driven parameters. All providers utilize consistent decorator interface ensuring DRY principles and extensibility. Error classification system uses custom exceptions for LLM, network, and provider-specific failures. Implementation avoids unnecessary dependencies while maintaining testability and maintainability. Design supports extensibility for future providers and advanced fallback logic, aligning with industry and research recommendations for robust LLM/HTTP error handling.
</info added on 2025-06-10T19:01:39.981Z>

subtasks.title: Post-implementation Validation: Test resilience mechanisms
subtasks.description: After implementation, write comprehensive unit tests for the resilience mechanisms. These tests should simulate failures and verify that the retry logic, backoff strategy, and circuit breaker states (closed, open, half-open) all function correctly.

tasks.title: Integrate Synthesis Engine with Existing Pipelines
tasks.description: Connect the content synthesis engine with the existing 5 pipeline types to enable optional AI enhancement
tasks.details: Modify BasePipeline to support optional synthesis step after chunk generation. Update all 5 pipeline implementations (PDF, HTML, DOCX, PPTX, Image) to integrate synthesis when --refine flag is enabled. Implement pipeline-specific synthesis optimizations and ensure backward compatibility with Phase 1 functionality. Add synthesis metadata to output chunks and maintain existing output format compatibility.
tasks.testStrategy: Pipeline integration tests for all 5 formats, backward compatibility verification, synthesis metadata validation, and output format consistency tests.

tasks.title: Implement Token Management and Cost Optimization
tasks.description: Create efficient token counting and cost optimization system for different LLM providers
tasks.details: Implement provider-specific token counting (tiktoken for OpenAI, approximation for others). Create TokenManager class with request batching, response caching, and context window optimization. Add cost estimation and tracking for cloud providers, token usage reporting, and efficient prompt engineering to minimize token consumption. Include cache management and token budget controls.
tasks.testStrategy: Token counting accuracy tests across providers, caching mechanism validation, cost estimation verification, and token budget enforcement testing.

tasks.title: Create Comprehensive LLM Integration Tests
tasks.description: Develop extensive testing suite for LLM integration with mocking, integration tests, and quality validation
tasks.details: Create mock LLM providers for unit testing, integration tests with real Ollama instances, and end-to-end synthesis workflow tests. Implement quality preservation validation tests, performance benchmarking for synthesis operations, and error scenario testing. Add test fixtures for different document types and synthesis levels, and create automated quality metrics collection.
tasks.testStrategy: Mock provider testing, integration test validation with local Ollama, quality preservation measurement, performance benchmark verification, and error scenario coverage testing.

tasks.title: Performance Optimization and Documentation
tasks.description: Optimize synthesis performance for production use and create comprehensive documentation for Phase 2 features
tasks.details: Implement streaming processing for large documents, optimize memory usage during synthesis, and add performance monitoring. Create comprehensive documentation for LLM integration, synthesis configuration, and troubleshooting guides. Add usage examples, best practices documentation, and migration guide from Phase 1. Include performance benchmarks and optimization recommendations.
tasks.testStrategy: Performance benchmark validation, memory usage testing, streaming processing verification, documentation accuracy review, and example code validation.

tasks.title: Implement OpenAI LLM Provider Integration
tasks.description: Create OpenAI provider implementation with API integration, token counting, and provider factory selection logic to enable cloud-based LLM synthesis capabilities.
tasks.details: Implement OpenAIProvider class in src/synthesis/providers/openai.py inheriting from BaseLLMProvider. Add required methods: generate() using OpenAI Chat Completions API with proper message formatting, get_token_count() using tiktoken library for accurate token counting, and validate_connection() for API key verification. Configure API client with user's API key from config, implement proper error handling for rate limits and API failures, and add support for different OpenAI models (gpt-3.5-turbo, gpt-4, etc.). Update ProviderFactory in src/synthesis/providers/__init__.py to include OpenAI provider selection logic with 'openai' identifier. Add configuration options for model selection, temperature, max_tokens, and other OpenAI-specific parameters. Implement proper async support and streaming capabilities if needed. Include comprehensive error messages for authentication failures, quota exceeded, and model availability issues. Add logging for API usage tracking and debugging purposes.
tasks.testStrategy: Create unit tests in tests/test_openai_provider.py with mock OpenAI API responses using pytest-mock. Test generate() method with various input types and verify proper message formatting and response parsing. Test get_token_count() accuracy against known token counts for different text samples. Mock API failures (authentication, rate limiting, network errors) and verify proper error handling and retry mechanisms. Test provider factory integration by verifying OpenAI provider can be instantiated with 'openai' identifier. Create integration tests with real OpenAI API calls (using test API key) to validate end-to-end functionality. Test different model configurations and parameter settings. Verify token counting accuracy matches OpenAI's actual usage. Test synthesis workflow integration by running content synthesis with OpenAI provider and validating output quality and structure preservation.

tasks.title: Integrate and Expand Prompt Management for LLM Synthesis
tasks.description: Refactor PromptManager to load prompt templates from the /prompts directory, expand prompt coverage, and optionally implement ContentValidator for automated quality checks.
tasks.details: 1. Integrate PromptManager with /prompts directory for dynamic prompt loading based on file type and refinement level. 2. Review and expand prompt files to ensure coverage for all formats and tasks. 3. (Optional) Implement ContentValidator for automated quality checks. Pre-validate with firecrawl_search for best practices, post-validate with tests, mypy, and ruff.

subtasks.title: Integrate PromptManager with /prompts directory for dynamic prompt loading
subtasks.description: Refactor PromptManager to load prompt templates from the /prompts directory based on file type and refinement level. Fallback to hardcoded templates if no file is found.

subtasks.title: Review and expand prompt files for all formats and tasks
subtasks.description: Ensure prompt files in /prompts cover all supported formats (pdf, html, markdown, etc.) and all relevant synthesis/refinement tasks. Add or update prompt templates as needed.

subtasks.title: Implement ContentValidator for automated quality checks (optional)
subtasks.description: Implement or stub ContentValidator methods for factual accuracy, structure preservation, information loss, readability, and semantic similarity. Integrate with pipeline if feasible.

tasks.title: Implement and Validate Full Dynamic Prompt Management and Content Validation
tasks.description: Ensure all synthesis code uses dynamic prompt loading, expand prompt template coverage, implement ContentValidator logic, and add/expand tests. Post-validate with mypy and ruff.
tasks.details: 1. Audit and refactor all synthesis code to use PromptManager and dynamic prompt loading. 2. Expand and verify prompt templates for all formats and tasks. 3. Implement at least one real check in ContentValidator. 4. Add or expand tests for new/changed logic. 5. Post-validate with mypy, ruff, and test suite.

subtasks.title: Audit and refactor all synthesis code to use PromptManager and dynamic prompt loading
subtasks.description: Ensure every code path that generates prompts for LLM synthesis uses PromptManager and loads templates dynamically from /prompts. Remove or refactor any hardcoded prompt logic.
subtasks.details:
<info added on 2025-06-12T11:59:30.343Z>
Refactor plan clarified: (1) Remove the old PromptManager from engine.py. (2) Update ContentSynthesizer to use the new PromptManager from prompt_manager.py, passing the prompts directory and using get_template/format_template for prompt construction. (3) Update all pipeline code (via BasePipeline and/or direct calls) to pass the correct format/task/refinement info to synthesis. (4) Ensure fallback to default/hardcoded prompt if template is missing. (5) Test integration to ensure all LLM-powered synthesis uses dynamic prompt loading.
</info added on 2025-06-12T11:59:30.343Z>

subtasks.title: Expand and verify prompt templates for all formats and tasks
subtasks.description: Review the /prompts directory and ensure there is coverage for all supported formats (pdf, html, markdown, etc.) and all relevant synthesis/refinement tasks. Add or update prompt templates as needed.

subtasks.title: Implement at least one real check in ContentValidator
subtasks.description: Implement a real validation method in ContentValidator (e.g., readability score or semantic similarity) instead of just stubs. Integrate with pipeline if feasible.

subtasks.title: Add or expand tests for new/changed logic
subtasks.description: Add or expand unit and integration tests to cover dynamic prompt loading, prompt coverage, and ContentValidator logic. Use mocks and ensure tests are fast and isolated.

subtasks.title: Post-validate with mypy, ruff, and test suite
subtasks.description: Run mypy (strict), ruff, and the test suite to ensure type safety, code style, and correctness after all changes.
